bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no prior offers or messages, I should open by targeting my maximum-utility bid. Since there is no evidence yet about the opponent’s preferences and no time pressure, I will hold all issues at my top value, valueC.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my opening offer. The opponent’s only bid is all valueA, which is very low utility for me and provides no evidence of meaningful concession yet, so I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues, especially issueF, issueE, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.04, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA offer and shown no concession, so I will make only a small additional concession on a low-importance issue while holding firm on the higher-weight issues C, D, E, and F.

I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small additional concession because it is still very early at t=0.06. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA bid and has not shown concession, so I will concede only on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the more important issues, especially issueF, issueE, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid that concedes only on my lowest-weight issues while preserving valueC on the more important issues C, D, E, and especially F. Time is still very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to move close to the opponent’s all-valueA position, and their repeated identical offers show no real concession yet.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.86, since it is early in the negotiation and there is no need to rush toward the opponent’s very low offers. The opponent has mostly held at all valueA but recently moved issueD to valueB, so I treat issueD as a possible tradeoff while holding firm on my highest-weight issues issueF, issueE, and issueC.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while acknowledging the opponent’s only meaningful movement so far: they raised issueD to valueB. Since they are otherwise holding firm at valueA, I will keep my most important issueF and other high-value issues strong, while making a measured concession on issueC to avoid repeating my last bid exactly.

I am still targeting a relatively high-utility bid because the negotiation is early at t=0.14, but I am acknowledging the opponent’s only real concession so far on issueD by keeping it at valueB. The opponent has otherwise held firm at valueA on most issues, so I concede on lower-weight issues A and B and partially on issueC while protecting my highest-value issues E and F.

I am targeting a still-high utility because it is early (t=0.16) and the opponent has mostly held firm at valueA, with only limited movement on issueD and issueF. Since issueA and issueB are low weight for me I have already conceded them, and I will make a further concession on issueD while holding firm on the more important issueE and issueF.

I am targeting a still-solid utility while making a small non-repeating concession pattern, since time is only 0.18 and there is no need to drop near the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent has mostly held at all valueA, with limited signals around issueD:valueB and most recently issueF:valueB, so I keep my highest-weight issueF at valueC but offer valueB on issueD and issueC as a measured compromise.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility while avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid; at t=0.20 there is still little need to make deep concessions. The opponent has mostly held at valueA, with only small signals on issueD, issueF, and most recently issueC, so I concede low-weight issueA/issueB and issueD while holding firm on the most valuable issueF and issueE.

I am targeting a utility around 0.78, maintaining strong positions on the highest-value issues for me, especially issueF and issueE, while making concessions on the lower-weight issues and partially on issueC/issueD. At t=0.22 there is still time, so I do not need to drop to the opponent’s very low offers, but their history shows willingness to move to valueB on issueD and issueC, so I combine those signals into a bid that may be more acceptable without sacrificing my key priorities.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.78, keeping my most important issues issueF and issueE at valueC while using lower/mid concessions elsewhere. At t=0.24 there is still time, but the opponent has mostly held at valueA with limited concessions, most recently offering issueD: valueC, so I incorporate that signal while conceding issueC to valueA. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around 0.70, conceding further because the opponent has begun moving from all-valueA toward valueB on issueE and issueF while previously showing valueC on issueD. Since time is still only 0.26, I will preserve my highest-weight issueF at valueC, but concede issueE to valueB and keep low-weight issues at valueA to improve acceptability.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70 while still keeping the most important issueF at valueC, since the opponent just signaled willingness there. Time is still early-mid at t=0.28, so I should not concede too deeply, but I can combine concessions the opponent has already shown separately on issueC, issueD, and issueE. The opponent has been mostly firm on valueA but has offered valueB/C on several individual issues, so this bid uses only valid values and tests a plausible package without repeating my last offer.

I am targeting a higher-utility bid around the early-mid stage rather than accepting the opponent’s still very low offers; their utility to me has risen only slightly and mostly through one-issue concessions. I am holding firm on the highest-weight issueF and using concessions they have already signaled on issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueE while leaving issueB at a low-cost concession. Since t=0.30 is not yet late, I do not need to collapse to their latest offer.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility around 0.78 by holding firm on the highest-weight issueF and keeping issueD at valueC, while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB. At t=0.32 there is still time, but the opponent has only made limited concessions; their recent bids show some willingness on issueC and issueF, and prior offers showed valueC on issueD and valueB on issueE, so this bid combines concessions they have signaled with protection of my core value.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78 because the negotiation is only at t=0.34, so there is no need to make deadline-level concessions yet. The opponent has mostly held low values for me but has signaled willingness on issueD=valueC and occasionally on issueF, so I concede the low-weight issues A/B and issueC while holding issueE and issueF high.

I am targeting a weighted utility of about 0.70, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier bids while still protecting my highest-weight issueF. At t=0.36 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s mostly valueA pattern; their latest offer signaled willingness on issueC=valueC, and earlier offers showed occasional flexibility on issueF and issueE. With no messages to consider, I am using the bid history only and conceding low-weight issueA/issueB plus issueD while keeping issueF strong.

I targeted a moderate utility around 0.70: still well above reservation, but with meaningful concessions since we are in the mid-game at t=0.38. The opponent has mostly held to valueA, but their recent offers signal some flexibility on issueC, issueD, and occasionally issueF, so I keep my highest-weight issueF at valueC while matching their latest concession on issueD and conceding low/medium issues toward valueA.

I targeted a solid mid-game utility by accepting the opponent’s latest concessions on issueC and issueE while still holding firm on high-value/high-weight issueF and important issueD. At t=0.40 there is time left, so I should not drop to their latest low-utility offer; their bids show gradual concessions on C, D, E, and F but no movement on issueB, so I concede low-weight A/B first.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility around 0.86 by keeping my highest-weight issueF at valueC while conceding the low-weight issues A and B to valueA. At t=0.42 there is some pressure to move, and the opponent’s latest valid offer shows willingness on issueD and issueE at valueC, so I am incorporating those while making only a moderate concession on issueC.

I am targeting a high but not maximal utility bid around the midgame, keeping issueF at valueC because it is my most important issue while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB to valueA. The opponent has begun offering concessions on issueC, issueD, issueE, and occasionally issueF, so I am combining their signaled flexibility on issueC/D/E with my need to hold issueF; there are no messages to adjust for.

I am targeting a still-strong mid-game utility by holding firm on the highest-weight issueF at valueC while conceding on low-weight issueA/issueB and partially on issueC/issueE. At t=0.46 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low issueF demand; their recent offers show willingness to give valueC on issueD and valueB on issueC/issueE, so I am matching much of that pattern while protecting my key issue.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.71, conceding on low-weight issueA and issueB and partially on issueC/issueD/issueE while holding issueF at valueC because it is my most important issue. The opponent has gradually moved away from all-valueA bids and most recently signaled willingness on issueF: valueC and issueC/issueD at valueB, so I combine those signals with a modest concession on issueD while keeping issueE at valueB. No natural-language messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer history.

I targeted a mid-game utility around 0.74 by using the opponent’s latest concessions on issueB, issueD, and issueE while holding firm on issueF, my highest-weight issue. At t=0.50 there is still time, so I should not accept their low issueF/valueA position yet; their offers show gradual concession despite no messages.

At t=0.52, I targeted a moderate utility concession rather than holding near my earlier high offers, while preserving issueF=valueC because it is my highest-weight issue and the opponent has just signaled willingness to offer it. The opponent’s recent offers have improved for me but still leave issueC and issueD low; I will concede on lower/mid issues while asking for issueD=valueB to keep the bid above reservation and avoid repeating my previous exact offer.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility while preserving issueF at valueC because it is my highest-weight issue. The opponent has begun conceding on several issues (including occasional valueB/valueC on D/E/F and valueB on A/C), so I will reciprocate on lower-weight issues while not dropping too far at t=0.54.

I targeted a mid-game utility around 0.78 by conceding fully on the low-weight issues A and B while preserving high value on the most important issueF and issueE. The opponent’s latest offer has improved substantially for me on issueE and issueF, suggesting room for agreement, but since time is only 0.56 I can still ask for a modest improvement on issueD rather than accepting their exact terms.

I targeted a still-strong utility around 0.78 while making a visible mid-game concession on issueC and accepting the opponent’s latest signal on issueB and issueD. At t=0.58 there is some time pressure but not enough to drop to their low-utility offers; their recent offers show willingness to move on issueB, issueE, and issueF, so I hold issueE and especially high-weight issueF at valueC.

I targeted a solid mid-game utility around 0.78 by holding valueC on the higher-value issues the opponent has recently shown willingness to grant, especially issueC and issueF, while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB. At t=0.60 there is some time pressure, so I moved close to their latest valid offer but improved issueD to valueB, which they have offered before. There were no natural-language messages, and the offer history suggests the opponent is gradually conceding on issueC, issueE, and issueF.

At t=0.62, I am targeting a solid but more conciliatory utility around 0.74 by holding firm on my highest-weight issueF while accepting several values the opponent just signaled in their latest offer. The opponent has gradually moved away from all-valueA and has offered valueC on issueC and valueB on issueE, so I preserve those concessions and avoid repeating my last bid while conceding low/medium-priority issues.

At t=0.64 I am targeting a solid but more deal-oriented utility around 0.82, keeping issueF at valueC because it is my highest-weight issue. The opponent’s latest offer improved significantly on issueB, issueC, and issueD, so I am matching those concessions while only asking them to move on issueF; there are no messages to weigh beyond the offer pattern.

I am targeting a strong but more conciliatory weighted utility of about 0.864, still protecting issueF as the highest-weight issue while conceding lower-value positions on issueA and a partial concession on issueD/E. At t=0.66 there is meaningful time pressure, and the opponent’s recent offers show willingness to give valueC on issueB/issueF and valueB on issueE, so this bid builds on those signals without repeating my prior offer.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.82 while making meaningful concessions because we are past the midgame at t=0.68. The opponent’s recent offers have improved substantially for me, especially on issueF and issueD, so I will hold those high-weight gains while conceding low/medium areas like issueA and issueE to improve acceptability.

At t=0.70 I am targeting a moderate concession bid with utility about 0.788, keeping issueF at valueC because it is my most important issue. The opponent’s offers have improved over time and their latest bid moved many issues to valueB, so I am matching that broader movement while changing only issueF to a value they have previously offered several times.

I am targeting a strong but realistic utility around 0.825, keeping the highest-weight issueF at valueC and accepting concessions mainly on lower-weight issueA plus moderate values on issueB, issueD, and issueE. At t=0.72 there is meaningful time pressure, and the opponent’s latest offer has improved substantially for me by including issueC:valueC and issueF:valueC, so I am making a near-compatible counter rather than holding out for all valueC. There are no opponent messages to weigh, and all observed offers use valid domain values.

I am targeting an additive utility of about 0.743, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier bids while still protecting my highest-weight issueF at valueC. At t=0.74, time pressure justifies moving closer to combinations the opponent has already signaled, especially their recent willingness to offer issueC/valueC, issueD/valueB or valueC, and issueF/valueC in some bids; there are no opponent messages to weigh. I concede issueE and issueB to valueA and only keep strong positions where they matter most to me.
